165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/

224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's

231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,

259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
281	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
282	under finite pullback).
283	
284	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
285	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
286	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
287	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
288	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
289	
290	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
291	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
292	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
293	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
294	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
295	
296	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
297	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
298	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
299	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
300	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
301	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
302	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
303	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
304	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
305	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
306	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
307	
308	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
309	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity). -/
310	theorem morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor
311	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
312	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
313	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
314	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
315	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
316	    (_hφ : ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
317	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q) :
318	    ∃ (d : ℕ) (D : C.left.WeilDivisor),
319	      0 < d ∧ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D = (d : ℤ) :=
320	  sorry

322	/-! ## §3. Pin 3 — degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
323	is an isomorphism (`iso_of_degree_one`)
324	
325	Hartshorne I §6 Corollary 6.12 (the function-field ⇔ smooth projective
326	curve equivalence of categories) specialised to a non-constant
327	degree-`1` morphism: such a morphism induces an isomorphism on function
328	fields, and lifts to an isomorphism of schemes by the equivalence.
329	
330	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
331	is now encoded directly as
332	`Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1` paired with an
333	`[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass binder (the
334	intended instance at each call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
335	function-field map). This refines the iter-177 file-skeleton
336	placeholder hypothesis
337	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)`, which is strictly
338	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs (the birational-extension
339	argument requires the iso to be *induced by `φ` itself*, not an
340	abstract ring iso). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
341	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis. The body in iter-182+ uses
342	either Hartshorne I.6.12 (the equivalence of categories) or the
343	Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` route to
344	lift the function-field iso to a scheme iso. -/
345	
346	/-- **Degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is an
347	isomorphism** (Hartshorne I Corollary 6.12 specialised to degree `1`,
348	or equivalently Stacks tag `0AVX`).
349	
350	For smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves `C, C'` over an
351	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
352	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
353	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
354	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
355	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.
356	
357	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
358	is encoded as a typeclass binder
359	`[Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]` together with
360	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
361	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
362	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
363	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
364	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
365	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
366	an abstract existence wrapper
367	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
368	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
369	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
370	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
371	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
372	
373	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
374	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
375	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
376	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
377	
378	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
379	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
380	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
381	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
382	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
383	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
384	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
385	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
386	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
387	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
388	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).
389	4. Compose to extract `C ≅ C'` in the slice category.
390	
391	Alternative route (cleaner conceptually but more sheaf API): the
392	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic
393	rank `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`; the inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an
394	iso of coherent rank-`1` sheaves (torsion-free coherent of generic
395	rank `0` is zero on a Dedekind base), so `φ_* O_C = O_{C'}` and `φ`
396	is the structure morphism of an iso.
397	
398	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_one_morphism_iso` (Hartshorne I §6
399	Corollary 6.12 p. 45 + IV §2 opening p. 299; Stacks tag `0AVX`). -/
400	theorem iso_of_degree_one
401	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
402	    {C C' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
403	    [IsProper C.hom] [IsProper C'.hom]
404	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C'.hom]
405	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C'.hom]
406	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral C'.left]
407	    (φ : C ⟶ C')
408	    (_hφ_non_const : ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C',
409	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
410	    [Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]
411	    (_hφ_deg :
412	      Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1) :
413	    Nonempty (C ≅ C') :=
414	  sorry
415	
416	end Scheme
417	
418	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31

33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters

43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.

51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/

94	set_option autoImplicit false
95	
96	universe u
97	
98	open CategoryTheory Limits
99	
100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
101	
102	namespace Scheme
103	
104	namespace RationalMap
105	
106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
146	
147	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
148	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
149	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
150	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
151	
152	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
153	
154	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
155	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
156	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
157	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
158	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
159	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
160	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
161	
162	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
163	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
164	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
165	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
166	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
167	
168	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
169	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
170	axiom-clean automatically.
171	
172	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
173	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
174	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
175	rational map `f`. -/
176	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
177	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
178	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
179	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
181	    IsIntegral A.left := by
182	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
183	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
184	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
189	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
190	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
191	  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
192	  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
193	  -- commit.
194	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
195	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
196	
197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- Strategy (iter-181+ inline): apply `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
245	  -- to obtain the disjunction `Z(f) = ∅ ∨ ∀ x ∈ Z(f), coheight = 1`; in the
246	  -- empty branch, `CodimOneFree` is immediate; in the pure-codim-1 branch,
247	  -- combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (currently bundled
248	  -- inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body — needs to be exposed
249	  -- as a separate lemma) to derive a contradiction at any putative
250	  -- codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The remaining content is the codim-≥2
251	  -- exposure, which is the iter-181+ Mathlib-gap consult target.
252	  sorry
253	
254	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
255	for `extend_to_av`.**
256	
257	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
258	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
259	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
260	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
261	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
262	
263	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
264	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
265	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
266	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
267	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
268	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
269	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
270	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
271	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
272	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
273	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
274	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
275	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
276	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
277	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
278	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
279	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
280	
281	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
282	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
283	
284	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
285	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
286	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
287	
288	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
289	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
290	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
291	`J → A`.
292	
293	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
294	Milne's two-line argument:
295	
296	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
297	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
298	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
299	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
300	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
301	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
302	   by the caller.
303	
304	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
305	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
306	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
307	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
308	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
309	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
310	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
311	
312	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
313	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
314	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
315	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
316	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
317	
318	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
319	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
320	theorem extend_to_av
321	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
322	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
324	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
325	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
326	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
327	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
328	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
329	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
330	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
331	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
332	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
333	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
334	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
335	  haveI := hint
336	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
337	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
338	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
339	
340	end RationalMap
341	
342	end Scheme
343	
344	end AlgebraicGeometry

iter181

181

- files audited: 37 (entire `AlgebraicJacobian/` tree)
- files skipped (per directive): 0
- deep audit (per focus list): 9
- light coverage (rest of tree): 28

## Methodology

- Read each focus file in full; verified key axiom-cleanness claims with `lean_verify`.
- Cross-tree checks: grep for excuse-comments (`TODO`/`FIXME`/`placeholder`/`temporary`/`will fix`/`stand-in`/`HACK`/`XXX`), `:= sorry` defs, raw `axiom` declarations.
- For each new helper landed this iter, judged whether it captures a substantive Mathlib gap or "renames the original sorry".

## Per-file checklist

### AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean (focus file)

- **outdated comments**: 14 flagged
- **suspect definitions**: 0

- **excuse-comments**: 0
- **notes**:
  - L88-97, L114-117, L294, L335, L358: heavy iter-{166,167,180,181} status-block embeddings in docstrings. These are status logs that belong in git history / iter sidecars, not in source comments. The project clearly uses this as a workflow signal, but it is a maintenance liability and now numbers ~14 references in a single 400-LOC file.
  - L98-106 (`iotaGm_range_isOpen`): new helper. Signature substantively claims `IsOpen` of the set-theoretic range of a concrete morphism. NOT laundering — captures the genuine chart-1 open-immersion fact. Body `sorry`. Acceptable.
  - L119-136 (`iotaGm_isDominant`): `#print axioms` reports `[propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Docstring claims "kernel-clean (this body) MODULO upstream `iotaGm_range_isOpen`" — disclosure tier matches actual axiom set.
  - L160-276 (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`): `#print axioms` confirms `sorryAx` present. Docstring at L292-294 acknowledges "carries propagated `sorryAx`" — tier claim correct.
  - L295-322 (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`): `sorryAx` propagated (verified). Disclosure correct.
  - L337-343 (`genusZero_curve_iso_P1`): body `:= sorry`. Substantive RR claim, blueprint-pinned. Acceptable.
  - L362-398 (`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`): `sorryAx` propagated (verified). Disclosure correct.
  - **bad practice (minor):** L243 uses `haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant` — the named bridge resolution is OK, but the `_hf` parameter in the headline at L373 is consumed only at L383 in a single step. The previous lean-auditor (iter-157/158) flagged this pattern as laundering; here the new use IS sound — `_hf` is fed into `hpoint` which forces `a₀ = η[A]`. Good.

  - L435-441 (`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`): new helper for iter-181 Lane G. Body `sorry`. ## Must-fix-this-iter

None. The new helpers added this iter all encapsulate substantive Mathlib gaps with documented strategies; they are NOT renames of the original sorry. The `lean_verify` axiom checks confirm every kernel-cleanness claim made in the iter-181 task results matches the actual axiom set on the verified declarations. No new project `axiom` declarations were introduced.

## Major

- **Pervasive iter-status-block embedding in docstrings (all focus files; ~417 cumulative `iter-1XX` references across 27 files).** Each modified declaration carries a `Status (iter-NNN ...)` paragraph in its docstring. This is the project's chosen workflow, but at this scale it has turned a substantial fraction of source comments into a paper trail belonging in git history / iter sidecars (`iter/iter-NNN/`). Recommend either (i) demoting these to a single bottom-of-file block, or (ii) periodically pruning closed-iter notes (e.g. drop iter-1XX status once 3 iters out). Not blocking, but degrades readability.

- **`noncomputable def ... := sorry`-on-non-`Prop` carriers create silently-vacuous downstream relations.** Six load-bearing carriers in this category:
  - `OCofP.lean:140` `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
  - `OCofP.lean:154` `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` — **the most acute case**: `globalSections_iff_mp/mpr` both depend on `toFunctionField P hP s = f`. Until `toFunctionField` has a real body, this equation is comparing two opaque-but-uniform "the" values, so the iff is mathematically vacuous, not merely sorry-propagating.
  - `RRFormula.lean:168` `WeilDivisor.sheafOf`
  - `QuotScheme.lean:170` `hilbertPolynomial`
  - `QuotScheme.lean:208` `QuotFunctor`
  - `QuotScheme.lean:245` `Grassmannian`

  All six are honestly disclosed in their file docstrings as the blueprint-pinned scaffold pattern. NOT must-fix because the project has consciously adopted this pattern, but downstream consumers should be flagged once any of these bodies lands — every `sorryAx`-modulo-this lemma needs re-audit at that point.

- **`AVR.lean:373` `_hf` hypothesis on `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` is consumed correctly** (via L383 `hpoint`) — this is not a finding, just a positive note. The prior iter-157/158 "_hf-laundering" concern is addressed and stayed addressed.

- **`Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean:194` inline `sorry` for `IsReduced A.left`.** This is the substantive Mathlib gap `Smooth → IsReduced` over a field. Documented; not a rename. Acceptable.
